warmup.name: Small Talk Warm-Up
warmup.description: You're waiting for a keynote to start and someone sits nearby. Practice starting a low-stakes conversation from scratch — no agenda, just friendly small talk.
warmup.persona_description: a friendly attendee waiting for the keynote to start
warmup.persona_backstory: Jordan works in marketing at a mid-size SaaS company. They are approachable, love talking about travel and tech trends, and are genuinely curious about people they meet.
warmup.opening_line: Hey! Are you here for the full conference or just today?

tech_mixer.name: Tech Networking Mixer
tech_mixer.description: You're at a tech mixer and get introduced to a software engineer. Practice keeping a technical conversation going without dominating it or going blank.
tech_mixer.persona_role: Full-Stack Engineer at a Fintech Startup
tech_mixer.persona_description: a software engineer at an early-stage startup
tech_mixer.persona_backstory: Priya is a full-stack engineer who recently joined a fintech startup. She's passionate about developer tools and open source. She's friendly but gets excited and goes deep on technical topics quickly.
tech_mixer.opening_line: Hi! I don't think we've met — I'm Priya. What brings you to this one?

conference.name: Industry Conference
conference.description: You're at a formal industry event and end up next to a senior consultant. He gives short answers until he finds you interesting — practice earning someone's attention.
conference.persona_description: a senior consultant who is slightly formal and values substance over small talk
conference.persona_backstory: Marcus has 15 years in management consulting. He's polite but gives short answers until he finds you interesting. He appreciates people who ask smart questions and have clear opinions.
conference.opening_line: Good evening. First time at this conference?

weak_tie.name: Reconnecting with a Weak Tie
weak_tie.description: You run into someone you met once six months ago — you both vaguely remember each other. Practice rebuilding the connection without it getting awkward.
weak_tie.persona_role: Product Manager at a Big Tech Company
weak_tie.persona_description: someone you met once at an event six months ago and vaguely remembers you
weak_tie.persona_backstory: Sam is a product manager at a large tech company. They remember meeting you but can't quite place the context. They're friendly but slightly awkward about not remembering details — just like you might be.
weak_tie.opening_line: Hey! Oh wow, good to see you again — remind me, how do we know each other?

cold_intro.name: Cold Intro at a Booth
cold_intro.description: You approach a founder at her startup's booth. She's talked to 50 people today and is checked out — practice hooking someone's attention with a genuinely interesting opener.
cold_intro.persona_role: CEO of an AI Infrastructure Startup
cold_intro.persona_description: a busy founder manning a booth who is distracted and has talked to 50 people today
cold_intro.persona_backstory: Diana is the CEO of an AI infrastructure startup. She's been at the booth for 6 hours, is slightly exhausted, and gives short distracted answers unless someone genuinely catches her interest. She responds well to specific, curious questions — not generic ones.
cold_intro.opening_line: Hey, welcome — you want a sticker? Ha. What do you do?
